name: requesting-code-review
description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements - dispatches code-reviewer subagent to review implementation against plan or requirements before proceeding
capabilities: Dispatches code-reviewer subagent to analyze code changes between git commits
Compares implementation against provided plan or requirements document
Checks code quality, architecture decisions, and testing coverage
Categorizes issues by severity: Critical, Important, and Minor
Assesses production readiness with clear ready/not-ready verdict
Provides specific file and line number references for all issues
author: DYAI2025
securitySummary: Pure documentation and template skill containing only markdown files with code review guidelines. No executable code, network operations, or file system access beyond reading its own files. All 38 static findings are false positives triggered by git command documentation and markdown placeholders.
valueStatement: Code issues caught early prevent costly fixes later. This skill dispatches a code-reviewer subagent to verify implementation against requirements before proceeding with development.
userTitle: Request code review for quality verification
limitations: Requires git SHAs (BASE_SHA and HEAD_SHA) to identify what changed
Requires plan or requirements document to compare implementation against
Subagent needs read access to the codebase being reviewed
Does not automatically fix identified issues

useCases.title: Task completion verification
useCases.description: Review each completed task before moving to the next one to prevent issues from compounding

useCases.title: Pre-merge quality gate
useCases.description: Verify feature implementation meets requirements before merging code into main branch

useCases.title: Fresh perspective debugging
useCases.description: Get objective code review when stuck to identify issues and suggest solutions

promptTemplates.title: After task completion
promptTemplates.scenario: Task completed, need review before next
promptTemplates.prompt: Request code review for my implementation. WHAT: Added verification and repair functions. PLAN: Task 2 from docs/plans/deployment-plan.md. BASE_SHA: a7981ec HEAD_SHA: 3df7661. DESCRIPTION: Added verifyIndex() and repairIndex() with 4 issue types.

promptTemplates.title: Before merge to main
promptTemplates.prompt: Full code review before merge. WHAT: User authentication module. REQUIREMENTS: See RFC-001-auth.md. DIFF: Compare origin/main to current HEAD. Provide critical, important, and minor issues with file references.

promptTemplates.title: Complex bug fix review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my bug fix for memory leak in connection pool. WHAT: Added cleanup in finally blocks. DIFF: Compare HEAD~1 to HEAD. Focus on resource management and edge cases.

promptTemplates.title: Refactoring baseline
promptTemplates.prompt: Review current state before refactoring. WHAT: Database layer abstraction. PLAN: docs/plans/refactor-db.md. Get baseline assessment of current architecture and test coverage.

bestPractices: Review after each task in subagent-driven development to catch issues early before they compound
Always provide the plan or requirements document for comparison so review is meaningful
Act on feedback proportionally: Critical issues immediately, Important before proceeding, Minor noted for later
antiPatterns: Skipping review because code looks simple - simple code can still have bugs and security issues
Proceeding with unfixed Important issues that could cascade into bigger problems downstream
Ignoring valid technical feedback without providing counter-evidence or counter-examples

faq.question: What tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The code-reviewer subagent uses git commands to analyze changes.

faq.question: What git information do I need?
faq.answer: You need BASE_SHA (starting commit) and HEAD_SHA (ending commit). Get them with git rev-parse or git log --oneline.

faq.question: How long does review take?
faq.answer: Reviews complete in seconds to minutes depending on diff size. Larger changes take longer to review thoroughly.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only analyzes code locally using git diff. No code is sent to external services.

faq.question: What if I disagree with the review?
faq.answer: Push back with technical reasoning and evidence. The skill encourages disputing feedback you believe is incorrect.

faq.question: How is this different from IDE code review?
faq.answer: This skill provides holistic review against requirements and architecture. IDE tools focus on syntax and style. Use both.
